meta.t001: Actual Budget — Curso completo para iniciantes
hero.t004: Um curso estruturado e amigável para iniciantes para entender os dois fluxos de orçamento do Actual e escolher o que melhor atende às suas necessidades.
hero.t009: Based on the official Actual docs so the tutorial stays close to the original source. Edit this tutorial on GitHub
lesson01.t004: Actual Budget is a privado, código aberto, local-first finance app. Esta lição explains what it is, why it's worth your time, and exactly how this course is structured so you always know where you are.
lesson01.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson01.t006: Saiba what Actual Budget is and what problem it solves
lesson01.t007: Entenda the idea of an Actual orçamento file
lesson01.t008: Saiba the difference between the Desktop Client and a Self-Hosted / Cloud Server
lesson01.t009: Have a clear map for the rest of the course
lesson01.t011: O que torna Actual Budget diferente?
lesson01.t012: Most orçamento apps are cloud services - your bank details and transação history live on a company's servers. Actual is diferente because it is local-first, código aberto, and does not require a mandatory hosted service. Você pode keep your orçamento on your own device, or run a servidor you control when you need sincronização and multi-device access.
lesson01.t014: When you first open Actual, you create a "orçamento file." Everything - your accounts, transações, categorias, and reports - lives in that file. It can stay local on your device, or sincronização across devices when you use a servidor. If you ever want to stop using Actual, you export your data and you own it completely.
lesson01.t016: Instalação: escolha um dos dois caminhos
lesson01.t017: Actual has two parts: the client, which is the app you use, and the servidor, which adds sincronização, Bank Sync, navegador access, and API access. The Desktop Client is the easiest way to start; a self-hosted or cloud servidor is better when you want Actual disponível across devices.
lesson01.t019: Easiest starting point for most beginners: download the desktop app for Windows, Linux, or Mac, create a orçamento, and start without Docker, hosting, or cloud setup.
lesson01.t021: Good for learning Actual and getting started quickly
lesson01.t022: Works offline and supports orçamento file export/import
lesson01.t023: Can optionally run a local servidor to enable Bank Sync.
lesson01.t024: Limit: Não app access remotely or from your phone
lesson01.t026: Best long-term setup for reliable remote web access from your phone, navegador, or anywhere. Você pode self-host it, or use a hosted option such as PikaPods.
lesson01.t029: Access Actual from navegador or phone navegador/PWA
lesson01.t030: Use Bank Sync where supported and configured
lesson01.t031: Use the Actual API and integrations
lesson01.t032: Connect the desktop app to your servidor
lesson01.t034: Start with the desktop app if you want the easiest setup. Learn Actual locally first, then move your orçamento later to a self-hosted or cloud servidor by exporting and importing your orçamento file. Escolha a servidor when you need reliable access from multiple devices, Bank Sync where supported, or API access. If you do not want to use the command line, PikaPods is the simplest hosted option.
lesson01.t036: What does a orçamento app actually do for you?
lesson01.t037: A orçamento app does not magically fix everything, but it gives you clarity. It helps you answer two important questions: where did my dinheiro go, and can I afford this with confidence?
lesson01.t038: In Actual, you do this through accounts, transações, categorias, and your mensal orçamento. Do not worry if those words are new; each one has its own lesson in this course.
lesson01.t040: You add your accounts, record or import transações, then assign each transação to a categoria like Groceries or Rent. After that, the orçamento page and reports start showing the real picture of your gastos.
lesson01.t043: This is a short 9-lesson course. The goal is not to memorize everything; it is to build your orçamento step by step without big jumps.
lesson01.t044: Foundation — What Actual is, the two orçamento modes, and which workflow fits you.
lesson01.t045: Core Setup — Build your orçamento, add accounts, and understand credit cards.
lesson01.t046: Daily Use — Handle transações and use rules to reduce manual work.
lesson01.t047: Insights & Improvement — Read reports, then use tips and FAQ to keep improving.
lesson01.t049: Read lições 01 and 02 first to understand the idea. Then go to lesson 03 and start building your actual orçamento. Do not try to configure every feature at the beginning; you will learn what you need at the right time.
lesson01.t052: You do not need to change anything right now. Just know that Settings is where you will return later to change orçamento mode, date and number formats, and other preferences.
lesson01.t053: 📍Settings — access it from the arrow next to the orçamento name or from More → Settings in the sidebar.
lesson01.t056: Starting Fresh — setting up a new file, accounts, and first categorias
lesson01.t057: Installing Actual — ways to run Actual with or without a servidor
lesson01.t058: Download Desktop App — download the desktop app from GitHub
lesson01.t059: PikaPods setup guide — run Actual with a simple hosted servidor
lesson01.t060: Settings — general preferences and orçamento method
lesson01.t062: Two Budgeting Modes: Which One to Start With
lesson02.t004: Actual offers two valid orçamento workflows. Envelope is Actual's padrão and recomendado dinheiro-allocation method, while Tracking is a lighter traditional forecast workflow for receita and expense targets. Escolha the workflow that fits your situation - Lição 03 will automatically adapt.
lesson02.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson02.t006: Entenda what Tracking Budget mode is and how it works
lesson02.t007: Entenda what Envelope (zero-sum) mode is and how it works
lesson02.t008: Escolha the mode that fits your need for control or awareness
lesson02.t009: Saiba how to activate your chosen mode in Settings
lesson02.t011: Actual padrão and recomendado dinheiro-control method
lesson02.t012: Orçamento only dinheiro you actually have. When receita arrives it becomes Available Funds, then you assign it to expense and saving categorias or hold it for next mês.
lesson02.t013: Shows whether you can afford gastos before it happens
lesson02.t014: Every dollar is assigned or intentionally held for next mês
lesson02.t015: Overspending requires funding or affects next mês's To Budget
lesson02.t019: Set mensal targets for expected receita and expected despesas, then compare reality against the plan. This workflow does not require you to conta for every disponível dollar or fund every overspend from another categoria.
lesson02.t020: Good when you already have a comfortable dinheiro buffer
lesson02.t021: Shows variance from target; does not reserve disponível dinheiro
lesson02.t022: Funds do not automatically roll over mês to mês
lesson02.t023: Your chosen mode — lighter planning and awareness
lesson02.t028: Available dinheiro: dinheiro you actually have now
lesson02.t029: Forecast: expected receita and expected expense targets
lesson02.t031: Receita becomes Available Funds only when received
lesson02.t032: You orçamento expected receita for the mês
lesson02.t034: You assign disponível dinheiro to expense and saving categorias
lesson02.t035: You set targets for what you expect to spend or save
lesson02.t037: Funding decision. Move dinheiro from another categoria, or let overspending reduce next mês's To Budget
lesson02.t038: Variance from target. Increasing the amount updates the forecast and lowers Projected Savings; it does not move real dinheiro
lesson02.t040: Categoria balances roll forward, and Hold for Next Month can reserve dinheiro you do not want to use now
lesson02.t041: Funds do not automatically roll over mês to mês
lesson02.t043: People who need dinheiro control and a clear answer to: can I afford this gastos?
lesson02.t044: People with a comfortable dinheiro buffer who want gastos awareness and receita/expense targets with lower maintenance
lesson02.t045: You've chosen Envelope Budget. This is Actual's padrão and recomendado method for controlling disponível dinheiro. Head to Lição 03 to see how it works.
lesson02.t047: How to enable Tracking Budget mode in the app
lesson02.t048: Actual starts in Envelope mode by padrão. If you choose Tracking as your receita-and-expense forecast workflow, change this setting before building the orçamento. Você pode change modes later, but do not use switching as the main plan because historical orçamento values do not always carry the same meaning between modes.
lesson02.t049: Settings → click "Switch to tracking orçamento." Do this before entering orçamento numbers so you start with the right workflow.
lesson02.t051: Click the arrow next to the orçamento name, or go to More → Settings in the sidebar.
lesson02.t053: It's near the top of the Orçamento section in Settings.
lesson02.t054: Done — the orçamento view updates immediately
lesson02.t055: The columns change from "To Budget / Available" to "Budgeted / Spent / Saldo."
lesson02.t058: Envelope orçamento — the padrão dinheiro-control workflow
lesson02.t059: Tracking Budget — the traditional receita/expense forecast workflow
lesson02.t060: Settings — where to change Budgeting Method
lesson03.t004: This is where you learn how the orçamento page works in each workflow. Use the toggle below to compare, but choose one mode in the app based on your needs.
lesson03.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson03.t006: Entenda how categorias organize your orçamento
lesson03.t007: Saiba how Envelope Budgeting uses To Budget
lesson03.t008: Saiba how Tracking Budgeting uses mensal targets
lesson03.t009: Entenda what overspending means in each mode
lesson03.t010: Design a simple starting categoria structure
lesson03.t014: Categorias: the buckets your dinheiro flows into
lesson03.t015: Every orçamento-impacting non-transferência transação should be assigned to a categoria. Categorias are how Actual knows what your dinheiro was spent on - without them, you have a list of transações with less meaning. This applies to both modes.
lesson03.t016: Actual lets you group categorias - "Groceries" and "Eating Out" can both sit inside a "Food" group. This keeps the orçamento page readable and lets you see group-level totals at a glance.
lesson03.t017: Receita categorias appear in the orçamento too, but Actual keeps them in one receita group. Adicione your receita sources there, such as Salary or Freelance.
lesson03.t019: On the Orçamento page, the left column is a list of your categoria groups, each expandable. You set a "Budgeted" amount next to each categoria. As transações come in and are categorized, the columns update automatically.
lesson03.t021: "To Budget": decide what to do with disponível dinheiro
lesson03.t022: In Envelope Budget, you do not orçamento projected receita. When receita actually arrives, it becomes Available Funds. Then you decide: assign it to expense and saving categorias this mês, or hold part of it for next mês with Hold for Next Month.
lesson03.t024: Click the Budgeted column next to each expense or saving categoria and enter an amount. Start with essentials like rent, food, and bills. If dinheiro remains that you do not want to use this mês, you can hold it for next mês. The practical goal is for To Budget to reach zero because every dollar has a clear decision.
lesson03.t055: At the top of the Envelope Budget page, the summary bar shows what you actually have for this mês: Available Funds, anything overspent from last mês, what you have Budgeted for this mês, and what you chose to Hold for next month. Those numbers roll up into To Budget or Overbudget.
lesson03.t057: what you have to allocate this mês
lesson03.t059: reduces what you can allocate now
lesson03.t061: what you assigned to categorias this mês
lesson03.t063: dinheiro you intentionally held for next mês
lesson03.t065: Above zero = remaining To Budget | Below zero = Overbudget
lesson03.t067: To hold dinheiro for next mês, click the To Budget amount and choose Hold for next month. Press Enter to hold all disponível dinheiro, or enter a custom amount. This is optional: you can leave To Budget alone, but held dinheiro leaves this mês and appears in next mês's To Budget.
lesson03.t068: When a categoria goes red in Envelope mode
lesson03.t069: Overspent a categoria? Cover it now or accept the impact later
lesson03.t070: This is the heart and power of Envelope mode. When a categoria is overspent, you have two clear options: move dinheiro from another categoria to cover it, or let the overspending reduce next mês's To Budget. This makes the impact visible before it surprises you later.
lesson03.t077: Because you're consciously deciding: is eating out worth sacrificing entertainment? That question is what changes gastos habits over the long term.
lesson03.t079: Surpluses roll over — building a cushion over time
lesson03.t080: If you spend less than budgeted in a categoria, the surplus rolls forward automatically to next mês and adds to that categoria's saldo. Over months, categorias like "Car Maintenance" build up enough to cover big repairs without any crisis.
lesson03.t082: When dinheiro arrives, assign disponível funds to categorias or use Hold for Next Month until To Budget reaches zero. Weekly, import and categorize transações. If a categoria overspends, fund it from another categoria or accept its impact on next mês. When a new mês starts, create a new orçamento or copy last mês's orçamento and adjust what you learned. At mês end, review Reports.
lesson03.t084: Categorias: the buckets your dinheiro flows into
lesson03.t085: Every orçamento-impacting non-transferência transação should be assigned to a categoria. Categorias turn a raw list of transações into meaningful information. Você pode group categorias (e.g. "Food", "Housing") for better organisation.
lesson03.t086: Receita categorias appear in the orçamento too, but Actual keeps them in one receita group. Adicione your receita sources there, such as Salary or Freelance.
lesson03.t087: Start with 10–15 broad categorias. After one mês of real data you'll know which need splitting.
lesson03.t089: Set mensal targets: your forecast for the mês
lesson03.t090: For each receita categoria (like "Salary"), enter what you expect to receive. For each expense categoria, enter your gastos target. These are forecasts, not contracts. Being wrong is fine - the goal is a baseline you can refine.
lesson03.t091: How to find good starting numbers:
lesson03.t092: Open 2–3 months of bank statements and estimate your average per categoria. If unsure, orçamento generously.
lesson03.t122: The mensal summary: your orçamento's scoreboard
lesson03.t123: At the top of the Tracking Budget page sits a summary bar comparing your budgeted amounts (the plan) against your actual amounts (reality).
lesson03.t126: What you planned to receive vs what actually arrived
lesson03.t129: What you planned to spend vs what you actually spent
lesson03.t134: When a categoria goes red in Tracking mode
lesson03.t135: Red means variance from the target you planned, not a dinheiro envelope that must be refilled. Você pode leave it as a review signal, or adjust the target if the original forecast was unrealistic.
lesson03.t139:
If you raise the target
Projected Savings drops
lesson03.t140: Changing the number in Tracking mode updates the forecast only.
lesson03.t141: Projected Savings will decrease, but no real dinheiro is reserved or moved from another categoria. If you need to know whether dinheiro is disponível before gastos, that is Envelope mode's job.
lesson03.t144: Start of mês: copy last mês's orçamento
lesson03.t145: Click the copy icon at the top of the orçamento columns. Adjust anything you know will be diferente.
lesson03.t147: Set aside 10 minutes. Rules will automate most of this after a few weeks.
lesson03.t149: Decide whether the target was unrealistic and should be updated, or whether gastos needs to come down for the rest of this mês or next mês.
lesson03.t151: The Gastos report shows which categorias consistently run over. Use this data to set better targets next mês.
lesson03.t153: Designing your categorias — a starting framework
lesson03.t154: The right categorias are personal, but here's a framework that works for most people. Start broad and refine. Fewer categorias is better to start - you can always split later.
lesson03.t156: Receita categorias live inside a single receita group.
lesson03.t161: Concrete home and utility costs that are easy to recognize.
lesson03.t167: Regular day-to-day gastos that usually happens every mês.
lesson03.t173: Recurring commitments, not just entertainment subscriptions.
lesson03.t179: Money you're deliberately setting aside for future costs or goals.
lesson03.t185: Flexible personal gastos that does not fit housing, diário living, bills, or savings.
lesson03.t190: Don't spend more than 10 minutes on categorias before you have real transação data. Set up something reasonable now, then refine it after your first import.
lesson03.t191: Returns and reimbursements: If a store refunds a purchase, categorize the refund back to the original gastos categoria instead of treating it as new receita. For reimbursements from another person, check the official docs for the right workflow depending on whether you fronted the dinheiro.
lesson03.t194: How Budgeting Works — To Budget, overspending, and the mensal workflow
lesson03.t195: Categorias — adding categorias/groups, merging, and notes
lesson03.t196: Returns and Reimbursements — when dinheiro should flow back to a categoria
lesson03.t198: Contas: Adding and Managing Your Money
lesson04.t003: Contas — Adding and Managing Where Your Money Lives
lesson04.t004: Actual needs to know about the accounts you spend from, and you can also add other accounts for net worth. Esta lição focuses on where dinheiro lives: On Orçamento vs Off Orçamento accounts, common conta types, how to add them, and how to record transfers between your own accounts.
lesson04.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson04.t006: Saiba the difference between On Orçamento and Off Orçamento accounts
lesson04.t007: Saiba how to add all your accounts with the right settings
lesson04.t008: Escolha the right conta type: checking, savings, dinheiro, credit card, or tracking
lesson04.t009: Entenda transfers between your own accounts and when they need a categoria
lesson04.t011: On Orçamento vs Off Orçamento: choose the right conta role
lesson04.t012: Actual makes a distinction between accounts that affect your mensal orçamento and accounts that are only tracked for net worth. Before adding any conta, ask: should gastos from this conta affect my orçamento, or do I only want to track its value?
lesson04.t013: That decision determines whether the conta should be On Orçamento or Off Orçamento. Everyday gastos accounts are usually On Orçamento, while investments, loans, and large assets are usually Off Orçamento because they are for tracking only.
lesson04.t014: ✅ On Orçamento — affects your mensal orçamento
lesson04.t015: Use this for accounts you spend from or use to pay despesas. Transações in these accounts are categorized and appear in your orçamento and reports.
lesson04.t020: 📊 Off Orçamento — tracking and net worth only
lesson04.t021: Use this for accounts or assets you want to include in net worth, but not for day-to-day categoria gastos.
lesson04.t026: You cannot change an Off Orçamento conta to On Orçamento later. Always double-check this setting. Use On Orçamento for any active credit card; only consider Off Orçamento if there will be no new purchases and the card will be closed after payoff.
lesson04.t029: When you first open Actual, a large Adicione Conta button is shown in the centre of the screen. Clicking it starts a simple wizard. Você pode add more accounts at any time using + Adicione conta at the bottom of the sidebar.
lesson04.t030: For each conta, enter the current saldo shown in your bank's app. Start from the beginning of the current mês, or another recent date, instead of going far back in history. That saldo becomes the opening transação in Actual.
lesson04.t031: 📍The Adicione Conta button — shown prominently when you first open Actual, or disponível in the sidebar at any time.
lesson04.t032: 📍The conta creation dialog — choose a name, conta type, whether it's On or Off Orçamento, and enter the current saldo from your bank's app.
lesson04.t033: 📍A completed conta form — clear name, correct type, On Orçamento selected for a checking conta.
lesson04.t034: 📍Once created, accounts appear in the left sidebar. On Orçamento accounts show at the top; Off Orçamento at the bottom.
lesson04.t036: Transfers: moving dinheiro between your own accounts
lesson04.t037: When you move dinheiro between two accounts you own - checking to savings, or paying a credit card - use a Transferência instead of a normal transação. If both accounts are On Orçamento, it does not need a categoria because the dinheiro stays inside your orçamento. If one side is Off Orçamento, the On Orçamento side needs a categoria.
lesson04.t039: In the Favorecido field, click Make Transferência and choose the destination conta. Actual automatically creates matching entries in both accounts - a debit in one, a credit in the other. They're linked: editing one updates the other.
lesson04.t040: 📍Click "Make Transferência" in the favorecido dropdown to see your accounts and choose the destination.
lesson04.t041: 📍A completed transferência — both entries are linked. Updating the note in one automatically updates the other.
lesson04.t043: When do I categorize a transferência?
lesson04.t044: A transferência means moving dinheiro between your own accounts. The categoria regra depends on the conta types: are both accounts On Orçamento, or is one of them Off Orçamento?
lesson04.t049: Money stays inside the orçamento; only the conta changes.
lesson04.t051: Money leaves the orçamento, so categorize the purpose.
lesson04.t053: Money enters the orçamento, so categorize the source/context.
lesson04.t055: Both accounts are outside the orçamento; no categoria is affected.
lesson04.t058: Contas & Transações overview — On/Off Orçamento accounts and adding accounts
lesson04.t059: Transfers — transfers between accounts and transferência categorias
lesson04.t061: Cartões de crédito: The Part Everyone Gets Wrong
lesson05.t003: Cartões de crédito: The Part Everyone Gets Wrong
lesson05.t004: More questions are asked about credit cards in Actual than any other topic. Esta lição explains the underlying concept first, then walks through a complete worked example so there are no surprises.
lesson05.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson05.t006: Entenda how Actual treats credit cards (the core concept)
lesson05.t007: Saiba what happens to your orçamento when you swipe the card
lesson05.t008: Saiba how to record credit card payments correctly
lesson05.t009: Entenda what to do if you carry a saldo
lesson05.t011: The mental model: credit cards hold negative dollars
lesson05.t012: In Actual, a credit card is just another conta - but it holds negative dinheiro. When you create the conta, its saldo is negative (what you currently owe). As you make purchases, the saldo gets more negative. When you pay the card, positive dollars from your checking conta cancel out the negative ones.
lesson05.t013: The most important thing to understand: your orçamento categorias are updated at the moment of purchase, not when you pay the bill. When you buy $50 of groceries on your Visa, your Groceries categoria decreases by $50 immediately. Paying the card later is just accounting - moving dinheiro between accounts.
lesson05.t014: Why this is better than how you might be thinking about it
lesson05.t015: Some people think of credit card gastos as "not real dinheiro yet" until the bill arrives. Actual disagrees - and rightly so. You've committed to that $50 the moment you bought the groceries. Recording it immediately means your orçamento always reflects reality, not a mês-old picture.
lesson05.t017: Tracing a purchase end to end
lesson05.t018: Let's trace exactly what happens when you spend $50 on groceries with your credit card, then pay the card at mês end.
lesson05.t019: Etapa 1: You buy $50 of groceries on your Visa
lesson05.t022: Your Groceries orçamento reduced by $50 right now. The Visa conta records that you owe $50 more.
lesson05.t023: Etapa 2: At mês end, you pay $50 to Visa from Checking
lesson05.t027: This is a Transferência - no categoria needed. Your orçamento was already updated at purchase time. Paying the card is just two accounts settling up.
lesson05.t029: Paying in full each mês — the full workflow
lesson05.t030: Adicione your credit card as an On Orçamento conta
lesson05.t031: Use On Orçamento for any active card. Only consider Off Orçamento if there will be no new purchases and the card will be closed after payoff. Enter the current saldo you owe as a negative number.
lesson05.t032: Make purchases — categorize each one when it appears
lesson05.t033: Each purchase is recorded against the relevant categoria. In Envelope mode it affects the categoria's disponível saldo; in Tracking mode it appears as actual gastos against the planned target.
lesson05.t034: When your statement arrives, reconcile the card conta
lesson05.t035: Click the 🔒 lock icon → enter the statement saldo → confirm each transação. This catches anything that might be missing.
lesson05.t036: Record the payment as a Transferência
lesson05.t037: In your Checking conta, add a transação. Set Favorecido to "Transferência to [Card Name]" and enter the statement amount. Não categoria needed.
lesson05.t038: 📍End-of-mês orçamento — every card purchase has been categorized. Não categoria is overspent, so the full statement can be paid.
lesson05.t039: 📍After the Transferência payment — the card saldo moves back towards zero. Orçamento categorias unchanged.
lesson05.t040: 📍The orçamento after payment — identical to before the payment. This confirms the concept: gastos was recorded at purchase time, not at payment time.
lesson05.t041: The golden regra: Do not rely on the card saldo alone. Watch how card purchases affect your categorias: in Envelope mode, make sure the categoria is funded; in Tracking mode, monitor actual gastos against the target.
lesson05.t043: If you carry a saldo (paying minimum payments)
lesson05.t044: If you can't pay the full statement saldo one mês, you are carrying debt. This is a more detailed workflow than this lesson, but the big idea in Actual is to separate old debt from newly funded purchases.
lesson05.t045: Crie a "Cartão de crédito Debt" categoria group, with one debt categoria per card.
lesson05.t046: Set those debt categorias to Rollover Overspending so the negative card saldo is not counted twice.
lesson05.t047: Categorize the starting saldo, interest/fees, and any purchases you will not pay on the next statement to that card's debt categoria.
lesson05.t048: Record the actual payment as a Transferência from Checking to the card, and always pay at least the statement minimum.
lesson05.t049: Because carrying debt has its own mensal steps and payment calculation, follow Actual's original guide: Carrying Debt.
lesson05.t052: Cartões de crédito and Actual Budget — the core mental model
lesson05.t053: Paying in Full — official example for paying the statement mensal
lesson05.t054: Carrying Debt — detailed workflow when you cannot pay the full statement
lesson06.t004: Actual is only as useful as the transação data inside it. Esta lição covers how transações get in, what each row means, splits, payees, tags, filters, duplicates, and reconciliation so your data stays trustworthy.
lesson06.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson06.t006: Saiba the main ways to get transações into Actual
lesson06.t007: Entenda Bank Sync and when to use it
lesson06.t008: Be able to import a CSV file from your bank
lesson06.t009: Saiba how to handle split transações, duplicates, and reconciliation
lesson06.t011: The main ways to get transações in
lesson06.t012: Escolha based on your bank's capabilities and how much automation you want. Most people end up using File Import as a semanal habit, or Bank Sync if their bank is supported.
lesson06.t014: Connect through a supported provider, then click Bank Sync when you want to fetch novas transações.
lesson06.t019: Download from your bank, import manually. 5 minutes once a week.
lesson06.t022: Type each transação. Useful for dinheiro gastos or when other options aren't disponível.
lesson06.t024: Why is OFX better than CSV? OFX files include a unique bank-assigned ID for every transação. Actual uses this ID to automatically detect duplicates — meaning you can safely import the same time period twice without getting duplicate entries. CSV files don't have this, so duplicate detection is less reliable.
lesson06.t027: Bank Sync is optional. If your bank and regional provider are supported, you can connect the conta and click Bank Sync to fetch novas transações. It does not remove the need to review: you still categorize transações, check Rules, and reconcile accounts.
lesson06.t029: You use the desktop app with its local servidor, or an actual-servidor setup
lesson06.t030: Your bank is supported through SimpleFIN, Pluggy.ai, or GoCardless for existing users
lesson06.t031: You want a faster semanal routine than downloading files manually
lesson06.t033: Start with a recent opening saldo; avoid unnecessary old history
lesson06.t034: Bank sincronização credentials are stored on the local servidor or the servidor you use
lesson06.t035: Sync is not fully automático; you click Bank Sync when needed
lesson06.t036: For a calmer start, create the conta with a correct saldo on a recent date, then enable Bank Sync. Reconciliação is easier when only novas transações arrive after your start point.
lesson06.t038: Importing a CSV file from your bank
lesson06.t039:
Log into your bank and download a transação file
Look under "Transação History," "Download Statement," or similar. Escolha OFX if disponível; CSV otherwise. Select a date range from the start of this mês.
lesson06.t040: In Actual: open the conta → click Import → select the file
lesson06.t041:
Map CSV columns to Actual's fields
Tell Actual which column in your CSV is the Date, which is the Favorecido name, and which is the Amount. The live preview shows how Actual will interpret each row.
lesson06.t042:
Fix date format if dates look wrong
Try diferente format options (DD/MM/YYYY vs MM/DD/YYYY). If despesas show as positive numbers, toggle "Flip amount."
lesson06.t043:
Click Import
Actual automatically avoids duplicating transações it has already seen.
lesson06.t044: 📍The CSV import screen — map each column to the right field. The preview shows how Actual will interpret the data before you commit.
lesson06.t046: Transação anatomy: what each row means
lesson06.t047: Once transações are inside Actual, your main job is review and cleanup. You do not need to edit everything, but you should understand the key fields so you can categorize, search, and reconcile confidently.
lesson06.t051: Favorecido — merchant, person, or transferência conta
lesson06.t054: Categoria — where gastos appears in the orçamento
lesson06.t056: Cleared — has it appeared at the bank?
lesson06.t058: Your simple routine: review novas transações, clean the favorecido when needed, choose the categoria, then let Rules learn from your decisions.
lesson06.t060: Split Transactions: one transação, multiple categorias
lesson06.t061: Sometimes one bank transação contains multiple kinds of gastos. Example: one supermarket trip includes groceries, household supplies, and a gift. Instead of creating separate transações, split the transação into child lines, each with its own categoria and amount.
lesson06.t062: 📍Split Transação controls — add split lines, distribute the remainder, or unsplit when needed.
lesson06.t063: Open the transação's Categoria and choose Split Transação
lesson06.t064: A child row appears under the original transação.
lesson06.t066: Give each part its own categoria and amount.
lesson06.t067: Make sure the total equals the original transação
lesson06.t068: The child rows must add up to the parent transação amount.
lesson06.t069: Be careful with bulk editing split transações. If you need to change one, open the split so you can see each child line clearly.
lesson06.t072: Three features that make managing transações much faster once you have real data coming in.
lesson06.t073: Payees — cleaning up ugly bank names
lesson06.t074: Banks give payees names like TARGET DEBIT CRD ACH TRAN CO ID:XXX15170. From More → Payees, you can rename them, merge duplicates, and set padrão categorias. Actual also learns from your renaming and creates Rules automatically - so you only clean up each favorecido once.
lesson06.t075: 📍More → Payees: search for all variations of a favorecido name to merge them into one clean entry.
lesson06.t076: 📍After merging — all variations become one favorecido. Click the name to rename it to something clean like "Morrisons."
lesson06.t078: Adicione #hashtags to any transação's Notes field to group transações that cross categorias. Useful for tracking the real cost of a holiday (flights in Transport, hotels in Accommodation, meals in Eating Out - all tagged #spain2025).
lesson06.t079: 📍Type # in the Notes field to add a tag — you'll see autocomplete for existing tags.
lesson06.t080: 📍The Tags management page (sidebar) — assign colours to tags and view all transações for any tag.
lesson06.t081: Bulk editing — update many transações at once
lesson06.t082: Check the box on the left of multiple transações, then use the toolbar to change categoria, favorecido, notes, conta, or delete them all at once. Use Filters first to narrow down to exactly what you want to change.
lesson06.t083: Bulk editing doesn't work well with split transações. Avoid selecting split transações when using bulk actions.
lesson06.t086: The Filter button above any transação list lets you narrow what you see using multiple stacked conditions. This is far more powerful than text search - you can combine date ranges, categorias, amounts, payees, and tags.
lesson06.t087: 📍The Filter dropdown — choose from date, categoria, favorecido, amount, conta, notes, and tags. Combine as many as you need.
lesson06.t088: 📍Multiple stacked filters — here filtering by a #tag + categoria + year to find the total cost of running a specific car in 2024.
lesson06.t089: 📍Save filters you use regularly — click "Unsaved filter" → "Save new filter" to give it a name.
lesson06.t092: If you enter a transação manually and then later import the same one from your bank, you'll have a duplicate. Actual gives you a way to merge them cleanly.
lesson06.t094: Select both duplicate transações, then press <kbd>G</kbd> or use the dropdown → Merge. Actual keeps the more reliable version (bank-synced wins over imported, imported wins over manual) and copies any missing information (categoria, notes) from the dropped one to the kept one.
lesson06.t095: 📍Select two matching transações and press G — Actual merges them into one, keeping the most reliable version.
lesson06.t097: Reconciliação: verifying your records against your bank
lesson06.t098: Reconciliação is the habit of comparing Actual's transação list to your official bank statement and confirming they match. It catches missing transações, accidental duplicates, and amounts you may have mistyped.
lesson06.t100: Click the 🔒 lock icon in the top-right of any conta. Enter your current bank saldo, then click Reconcile. Actual shows you a "Difference" number. Go through transações ticking the ones that appear on your statement (grey circle = unconfirmed, green = confirmed). When Difference reaches $0, click Lock transações to lock the matched transações.
lesson06.t101: 📍Click the green saldo in the conta header to see Cleared vs Uncleared totals — helps you know where the discrepancy is before you start reconciling.
lesson06.t102: You don't need to reconcile every day. Once a mês is fine for most people — just make sure you do it at least mensal if you import transações manually.
lesson06.t105: Importing Transações — Bank Sync, file import, CSV, and manual entry
lesson06.t106: Connecting Your Bank — Bank Sync and supported providers
lesson06.t107: Merging Duplicate Transações — duplicate merging and kept-transação logic
lesson06.t108: Split Transactions — splitting one transação across categorias
lesson06.t109: Payees, Tags, Bulk Actions, and Filters — transação cleanup and management tools
lesson06.t110: Reconciliação — matching accounts and locking transações
lesson06.t111: Transfers — important when importing both sides of a transferência
lesson06.t113: Rules & Automation: Let Actual Do the Work For You
lesson07.t003: Rules & Automation: Let Actual Do the Work For You
lesson07.t004: Once your transações are coming in and getting cleaned up correctly, use Rules and Schedules to reduce repetitive work. Rules clean and categorize imported transações, while Schedules help you see recurring and expected transações before they happen.
lesson07.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson07.t006: Entenda what rules are and how they work
lesson07.t007: Saiba how Actual creates rules automatically from your behaviour
lesson07.t008: Saiba how to view, edit, and create rules manually
lesson07.t009: Entenda regra stages and how Schedules support automation
lesson07.t011: What a regra is: an "if → then" instruction
lesson07.t012: Every time a transação is imported, Actual runs it through all your rules in order. A regra has two parts: conditions (when to fire) and actions (what to change). If all conditions match, all actions are applied.
lesson07.t013: The most common use is cleaning up ugly favorecido names and setting categorias automatically. For example:
lesson07.t014: If all these conditions are true…
lesson07.t021: This turns AMAZON.COM*5C7QC7MH0 AM 10/26 into "Amazon / Shopping" every single time - automatically.
lesson07.t023: Actual writes most rules for you automatically
lesson07.t024: You don't need to build your regra library from scratch. Actual watches your behaviour and creates rules based on what you do:
lesson07.t025:
When you rename a favorecido
Actual asks: "Apply this rename automatically in future?" Click yes and it creates a pre-stage regra. That ugly bank name will never appear again.
lesson07.t026:
When you categorize a transação
After you assign the same favorecido to a categoria a couple of times, Actual creates a regra to apply that categoria automatically going forward. After a few weeks of use, most transações categorize themselves on import.
lesson07.t027: The payoff: After a mês or two of regular use, importing a week's worth of transações takes about 2 minutes — Actual cleans up the names, assigns categorias, and you just review and confirm. This is what makes the system sustainable long-term.
lesson07.t037: Matches any item in a list
lesson07.t043: Conditions can check: imported favorecido, favorecido, conta, categoria, date, notes, amount (inflow or outflow). All string matching is case-insensitive.
lesson07.t044: Actions can set: favorecido, categoria, notes, conta, date, amount, cleared status. They can also append or prepend text to notes.
lesson07.t047: Rules run in three stages. Knowing this prevents confusion when multiple rules affect the same transação.
lesson07.t048: Pre-stage runs first, always. Actual automatically puts favorecido-renaming rules here. This ensures favorecido names are cleaned up before any categorization rules run.
lesson07.t049: Default-stage is where most rules live. Within this stage, more specific rules (using is) automatically rank after broader ones (using contains), so the specific regra always wins.
lesson07.t050: Post-stage runs last, always. Use this for rules that must override everything else. Most users never need this.
lesson07.t053:
Go to More → Rules in the sidebar
See all your rules, including the ones Actual created automatically for you.
lesson07.t054:
Click "edit" on any regra to view it in detail
The editor shows a live list of existing transações that match your conditions — excellent feedback before saving.
lesson07.t055:
Select transações and click "Apply actions" to backfill
Retroactively apply a regra to past transações — great for re-categorizing a batch of old records in one click.
lesson07.t056: The regra editor is also a powerful bulk editor. Crie a temporary regra with conditions that match what you want to change, apply actions to selected transações, then delete the regra. Much faster than editing transações one by one.
lesson07.t059: Schedules help you see upcoming transações before they happen: salary, rent, subscriptions, loan payments, or credit card autopay. They can be recurring or one-time, and can be entered automatically or left for manual approval.
lesson07.t060: 📍Schedules overview — recurring or expected transações appear here so you can see what is coming before it happens.
lesson07.t067: Show upcoming items in the conta before payment date
lesson07.t068: Reduce manual entry for recurring transações
lesson07.t069: Can be linked with Rules for categorias and notes
lesson07.t071: Start with only three or four schedules: salary, rent, key subscriptions, and card autopay if you use it. Adicione more when you notice a recurring pattern.
lesson07.t074: Rules — conditions, fields, stages, and automático rules
lesson07.t075: Rules Examples — custom rules and Bank Sync transferência examples
lesson07.t076: Schedules — recurring and expected transações
lesson07.t077: Rule Action Templating — experimental advanced regra actions
lesson07.t079: Reports: Understanding What Your Data Is Telling You
lesson08.t003: Reports: What Your Data Is Telling You
lesson08.t004: Reports turn your categorized transações into honest insights about your financial life. After a mês or two of real data, this becomes the most valuable part of Actual.
lesson08.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson08.t006: Saiba which reports and cards Actual provides
lesson08.t007: Saiba when to use Cash Flow, Net Worth, and Gastos Analysis
lesson08.t008: Saiba when Calendar cards and Custom Reports are useful
lesson08.t009: Entenda the experimental reports at a high level
lesson08.t011: The Reports dashboard: your financial mirror
lesson08.t012: The Reports section is separate from the orçamento page. Where the orçamento page tells you what you planned to spend, Reports tell you what actually happened. After even a mês of use, the reports reveal things about your gastos that will surprise you.
lesson08.t013: 📍The Reports Dashboard — multiple widgets give you a complete financial overview. The dashboard is fully customizable; you can add, remove, and resize widgets.
lesson08.t014: Small addition: besides the graphs and reports below, the Reports dashboard can also include Summary cards for a quick number and Text widgets for notes or reminders on the dashboard.
lesson08.t016: Cash Flow: "Did I save dinheiro this mês?"
lesson08.t017: Cash Flow only looks at On Orçamento accounts, showing their saldo over time along with separate receita and expense visualizations. It gives you a picture of how receita and gastos affected your disponível dinheiro.
lesson08.t018: 📍Cash Flow — focuses on budgeted accounts, meaning dinheiro disponível for your orçamento.
lesson08.t020: In Tracking mode, compare dinheiro flow against your Projected Savings. If Projected Savings said $550 but dinheiro flow shows $200 growth, somewhere $350 went uncategorized or was undercounted. This nudges you to investigate.
lesson08.t023: Net worth is everything you own minus everything you owe - dinheiro, investments, property equity, minus debts, mortgages, and loans. It's the single most important number in your financial life, and it should trend upward over time.
lesson08.t024: 📍Net Worth over time — includes all accounts (On Orçamento and Off Orçamento). Adicione your mortgage and investment accounts as Off Orçamento accounts to see the full picture.
lesson08.t026: Gastos Analysis: expense trends over time
lesson08.t027: Gastos Analysis tracks and compares your despesas over a selected period. Use it to see trends, fluctuations, and areas of high gastos or savings - not just the single biggest categoria you spent on.
lesson08.t028: 📍Gastos Analysis — compare despesas across a selected period to spot trends, fluctuations, and areas of high gastos or savings.
lesson08.t029: How to use this report for better orçamento
lesson08.t030: After 2–3 months, use this report to ask: which despesas are rising, fluctuating, or showing a savings opportunity? Use that insight to refine Tracking mode targets or adjust Envelope mode categoria funding.
lesson08.t032: Calendar card: receita and despesas by day
lesson08.t033: The Calendar card displays diário receita and despesas in a calendar format for a selected period. Use it to spot payday, rent, subscriptions, or any days that are unusually heavy on the orçamento.
lesson08.t034: 📍Calendar card — a quick diário view of receita and despesas.
lesson08.t036: Custom Reports: when the question is specific
lesson08.t037: Custom Reports let you analyze transações as a table or chart: Bar, Line, Area, Donut, or Table. Você pode analyze Payments, Deposits, or Net, split results by categoria, group, favorecido, conta, or mês, and save the report back to the Reports page.
lesson08.t038: 📍Custom report examples — bar, line, area, donut, and table views. Build once, save to the dashboard.
lesson08.t039: Tip: Combine custom reports with tags for powerful specific analysis. Tag all holiday gastos with #holiday2025, then build a custom report filtered to that tag — instant holiday orçamento summary across all categorias and accounts.
lesson08.t041: Experimental Reports: advanced reports for later
lesson08.t042: These reports are experimental, so they may have bugs, missing functionality, or change later. Do not start here in your first mês; read about them when your data is more mature and you want deeper analysis.
lesson08.t044: A financial-planning report inspired by Your Money or Your Life. It estimates when passive investment receita may cover projected despesas using expense categorias, receita or investment accounts, a safe withdrawal rate, and an expense projection method.
lesson08.t046: Tracks Orçamento page numbers over time: Budgeted, Spent, Overspending Adjustment, and cumulative Saldo. It includes orçamento categorias only, not transfers or Off Orçamento accounts.
lesson08.t049: Cash Flow graph — budgeted accounts and disponível-dinheiro movement
lesson08.t050: Net Worth graph — all accounts and net worth
lesson08.t051: Gastos Analysis — expense trends and high-gastos areas
lesson08.t052: Calendar card — diário receita and despesas
lesson08.t053: Custom Reports — tables, charts, filters, and saving reports
lesson08.t054: Crossover Point report — experimental financial-planning report
lesson08.t055: Orçamento Analysis report — experimental orçamento-number analysis
lesson09.t004: You've completed the core course. This final lesson covers power user shortcuts, answers the most common questions, and gives you a clear framework for choosing the orçamento workflow that fits you long term.
lesson09.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson09.t006: Saiba a simple semanal habit to keep your numbers trustworthy
lesson09.t007: Have answers to the most common beginner questions
lesson09.t008: Have a clear framework for choosing between Tracking and Envelope
lesson09.t009: Saiba where to go when you have questions beyond this course
lesson09.t011: Keep Actual Simple: Your Weekly Rhythm
lesson09.t012: The goal is not to build a perfect system. The goal is to keep your numbers trustworthy enough to make better gastos decisions. A simple semanal habit is better than a complex setup you stop using.
lesson09.t015: Import, sincronização, or enter novas transações.
lesson09.t017: Revise overspending before it becomes invisible.
lesson09.t018: Check transfers between your own accounts.
lesson09.t022: Creating too many categorias on day one.
lesson09.t023: Using Tracking mode when you actually need dinheiro control.
lesson09.t024: Categorizing credit card payments when both accounts are On Orçamento.
lesson09.t025: Ignoring uncategorized transações for too long.
lesson09.t026: Judging reports before you have a full mês of data.
lesson09.t027: Simple regra: if you open Actual once a week and clean up uncategorized transações, transfers, and overspending, the orçamento stays useful without becoming a diário burden.
lesson09.t029: Which orçamento workflow fits you long term?
lesson09.t030: Do not treat Tracking and Envelope as two steps in one graduation path. Both are valid, but they answer diferente questions: do you want to compare plan vs reality, or control disponível dinheiro before gastos?
lesson09.t032: You have a comfortable dinheiro buffer and are not worried about running out of dinheiro before mês end
lesson09.t033: You want receita and expense targets, then a clear plan-vs-reality comparison
lesson09.t034: You prefer lower maintenance and do not want to continually assign every disponível dollar
lesson09.t035: You are comfortable treating red values as target variance, not a dinheiro-funding decision
lesson09.t037: Você precisa dinheiro control and want to know whether you can afford gastos before it happens
lesson09.t038: You want every dollar to have a clear decision: assigned now or held for next mês
lesson09.t039: You want overspending to force a funding choice or reduce next mês's To Budget
lesson09.t040: You are comfortable maintaining the orçamento during the mês, not only reviewing it afterward
lesson09.t041: The practical decision: there is no required graduation path from Tracking to Envelope. Escolha based on your need for control and the maintenance level you accept. Você pode change modes later, but do not rely on switching as the learning plan because historical orçamento values do not always carry the same meaning between modes.
lesson09.t044: This is not the full official shortcut list; it is the useful diário set. Open <kbd>?</kbd> inside Actual whenever you need the complete list. On macOS, use <kbd>Cmd+K</kbd> for the Command Palette.
lesson09.t049: Open the Command Palette to search commands and jump to accounts
lesson09.t051: Open the Help Menu with docs, support, and the full shortcut list
lesson09.t053: Toggle the Privacy Filter to hide amounts on screen
lesson09.t055: Undo or redo changes in the current session
lesson09.t057: Import transações into the current conta
lesson09.t059: Initiate Bank Sync when the conta is connected
lesson09.t061: Adicione a new transação with the date picker open
lesson09.t063: Open Filter when none are selected, or show only selected transações
lesson09.t065: While editing: save the value and move down or up
lesson09.t067: While editing: save the value and move right or left
lesson09.t071: Toggle cleared status for selected transações
lesson09.t073: Link or view schedule for the selected transação
lesson09.t075: Merge two selected transações when their amounts are equal
lesson09.t076: Full official reference: Keyboard Shortcuts.
lesson09.t077: Some bulk-action shortcuts require one or more transações to be selected first. If a shortcut does nothing, select the transação and try again.
lesson09.t078: macOS note: the docs list Cmd+K for Command Palette on macOS. Other shortcuts may depend on navegador/app behavior, so use ? inside Actual to confirm what is disponível in your setup.
lesson09.t080: Operational topics you will need later
lesson09.t081: These are not part of your diário routine, but they matter once Actual becomes your real financial system. Saiba they exist now, and come back when needed.
lesson09.t083: Before major updates or trying new features, export a backup from Settings. It is not a diário task, but it is an important safety net.
lesson09.t085: The desktop app runs locally without an external servidor, which is fine for getting started. But if you want phone access or multiple devices, a sincronização servidor is the better experience. Sync is not a full replacement for backups.
lesson09.t087: Actual does not currently support native multi-currency. If you have accounts in multiple currencies, read the docs before adding them so you choose the right workaround.
lesson09.t090: Experimental features are optional tools that are still being developed. They may be incomplete, change later, or be removed. Do not make them the foundation of your beginner workflow.
lesson09.t092: Settle accounts, categorias, importing, Rules, and reconciliation before enabling experiments.
lesson09.t094: This matters especially before trying Budget Templates or Rule Action Templating.
lesson09.t095: Enable one feature at a time
lesson09.t096: Entenda its effect on your orçamento before adding another one.
lesson09.t097: Most useful later: Budget Templates for filling orçamento amounts, Rule Action Templating for advanced rules, and experimental reports for deeper analysis.
lesson09.t100: P: Do I have to categorize every single transação?
lesson09.t101: R: Sim - in both modes, every orçamento-impacting non-transferência transação needs a categoria. Off Orçamento transações are not categorized, and transfers between On Orçamento accounts do not need categorias. Categorias make reports useful and keep the orçamento working. The good news: Rules handle most categorization automatically after a few weeks.
lesson09.t102: P: In Tracking mode, do unused orçamento amounts carry over to next mês?
lesson09.t103: R: Não. This is a fundamental difference from Envelope mode. In Tracking mode, each mês starts fresh - you set new targets each mês (or copy last mês's with one click). In Envelope mode, leftover dinheiro in categorias rolls forward automatically and builds up over time.
lesson09.t104: P: Can I sincronização my bank?
lesson09.t105: R: Sim, if your setup and provider are supported. Actual supports SimpleFIN for North America, Pluggy.ai for Brazil, and GoCardless BankAccountData for existing EU/UK users. Bank Sync works with the desktop app through its bundled local servidor, or with an actual-servidor setup, and you click Bank Sync manually to fetch novas transações. Provider credentials are stored on the local servidor or the servidor you use.
lesson09.t106: P: Is there a aplicativo móvel?
lesson09.t107: R: The dedicated mobile apps are no longer maintained. Use Actual in your phone's navegador - the interface is responsive and works well. On iOS, tap Share → "Adicione to Home Screen" for a full-screen app experience. On Android, use "Adicione to Home Screen" from the navegador menu.
lesson09.t108: P: What if I overspend a categoria badly in Tracking mode?
lesson09.t109: R: Treat it as variance from the target. Você pode leave the red value as a reminder that the plan did not match reality, or increase the target if the original forecast was unrealistic. If you increase the target, Projected Savings decreases, but no dinheiro is reserved or moved from another categoria.
lesson09.t110: P: How do I update Actual after a new version is released?
lesson09.t111: R: It depends on how you run Actual. If you use the desktop app, update the app when a new release is disponível. PikaPods auto-updates around a week after each release. Docker users pull the new image. Fly.io has a specific update guide in the official docs at actualbudget.org/docs.
lesson09.t113: You now have everything you need to use Actual Budget confidently. The rest comes from building the semanal habit and letting the data accumulate.

mode.choice.envelope: Você escolheu Envelope Budget. Este é o fluxo padrão e recomendado do Actual para controlar o dinheiro disponível. Nenhuma alteração de configuração é necessária.
mode.choice.tracking: Você escolheu Tracking Budget. Este é o fluxo tradicional de previsão de receitas e despesas. Como o Actual começa em Envelope por padrão, altere uma configuração: Settings → Switch to tracking budgeting.
errors.lessonLoadTitle: Não foi possível carregar a lição
errors.lessonLoadMessage: Atualize a página ou verifique se o arquivo da lição existe em assets/lessons/.
errors.screenshotUnavailable: Captura de tela indisponível. Abra a documentação vinculada do Actual se precisar da imagem mais recente da interface.
